description_long: This is a vintage wooden stereo sound system console with a rectangular form and mid-century modern design. The front features two large speaker grilles with a diamond lattice pattern on either side of three central cabinet doors, each with a circular pull handle. The wood is a warm, reddish-brown tone, and the unit stands on four tapered legs, giving it a slightly elevated profile. The back panel is perforated with ventilation holes and includes several ports and labels, indicating access for power and audio connections. The sides are plain, showcasing the wood grain, while the top surface is flat and expansive, suitable for placing decorative items. A small metallic brand plate is visible on the lower right of the front panel.
description: A vintage wooden stereo console with diamond-patterned speaker grilles, three central cabinet doors, and a flat top, standing on tapered legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the diamond-patterned speaker grilles, three cabinet doors with circular handles, and a small metallic brand plate.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wooden panel and the depth of the console, with the legs partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a perforated back panel with multiple ports, labels, and ventilation holes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wooden side and the console's depth.
attribute_brand_logo: A small metallic rectangular plate on the lower right of the front panel, with unreadable text.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Diamond lattice pattern with intersecting diagonal lines forming a mesh over the speaker areas on both sides of the front panel.
attribute_control_panel_layout: No visible control panel on the exterior; possible controls may be behind the central cabinet doors.
